The only correct answer is D (butane-1,4-diol) A is incorrect because this compound is held together by London forces only B is incorrect because this compound is held together by London forces only C is incorrect because this compound is held together by hydrogen bonds, but only one per molecule (1) Number Answer Mark The only correct answer is A (Mg + H O → MgO + H ) B is incorrect because magnesium oxide is formed, not magnesium hydroxide C is incorrect because magnesium oxide is formed, but is MgO not Mg O D is incorrect because magnesium oxide is formed, not magnesium hydroxide, which is Mg(OH) (1) Choose an item. Number Answer Mark The only correct answer is D ( ) A is incorrect because both nitrates form an oxide, nitrogen dioxide and oxygen B is incorrect because lithium oxide, nitrogen dioxide and oxygen are formed C is incorrect because calcium oxide, nitrogen dioxide and oxygen are formed (1) Number Answer Mark The only correct answer is A (gaps between electronic energy levels) B is incorrect because the ionic radius plays no part in the colour of the flame test C is incorrect because the electrons move between energy levels and are not lost D is incorrect because it is not the number of electrons but the energy gap between levels which is important (1) lithium oxide, nitrogen dioxide and oxygen calcium oxide, nitrogen dioxide and oxygen Number Answer Mark The only correct answer is D (strontium chloride) A is incorrect because the precipitate would be yellow, not white B is incorrect because the precipitate would be cream coloured, not white C is incorrect because the flame test would be lilac not red (1) Number Answer Mark The only correct answer is D (C H O) A is incorrect because this compound requires 8 moles of oxygen B is incorrect because this requires 8.5 moles of oxygen C is incorrect because this requires 9.5 moles of oxygen (1) Number Answer Mark The only correct answer is C (sodium nitrate solution) A is incorrect because a white precipitate

The only correct answer is B (red to orange) A is incorrect because the end-point is orange and the sulfuric acid solution with methyl orange would be red C is incorrect because the end-point is orange and the sulfuric acid solution with methyl orange would be red D is incorrect because the end-point is orange and the sulfuric acid solution with methyl orange would be red (1) Number Answer Mark

The only correct answer is C (0.29) A is incorrect because this is the number of moles of potassium sulfate formed B is incorrect because this is the number of moles of potassium ions in the potassium sulfate formed D is incorrect because this is the concentration of the potassium ions in the final solution (1) Number Answer Mark A is incorrect because the percentage uncertainty is the same for sulfuric acid and potassium hydroxide (0.4%) B is incorrect because the percentage uncertainty for the pipette reading of sulfuric acid has assumed two readings D is incorrect because the percentage uncertainty for the burette reading of potassium hydroxide has assumed one reading (1) the total percentage uncertainty is 0.8% Number Answer Mark The only correct answer is D (number of electrons in the molecule increase) A is incorrect because halogen molecules are not polar B is incorrect because the general trend is for a decrease in bond strength and does not affect boiling temperature C is incorrect because electronegativity decreases down the group and is not relevant to bond strength in halogens (1) Number Answer Mark The only correct answer is C (the first ionisation energy of Cl is greater than that of Br) A is incorrect because the atomic radius of isotopes is the same B is incorrect because the electronegativity of isotopes is the same D is incorrect because the mass spectrum of this molecule would have three molecular ions peaks (1) Number Answer Mark 14 The only correct answer is B ( yellow purple) A is incorrect because although the lower layer would be brown if concentrated enough the upper layer is purple C is incorrect because the upper layer should be purple D is incorrect because the colours would be correct in a concentrated enough solution if swapped (1) Number Answer Mark

The only correct answer is C (E mode and E mean only) A is incorrect because E a would not be changed by temperature B is incorrect because E mean would also increase D is incorrect because E a would not be changed by temperature (1) Number Answer Mark

The only correct answer is D (E a , E mode and E mean ) A is incorrect because all three would decrease as there are fewer particles in the volume B is incorrect because all three would decrease as there are fewer particles in the volume C is incorrect because all three would decrease as there are fewer particles in the volume (1) Number Answer Mark

The only correct answer is A (E a only) B is incorrect because the number of particles at E mode and the value of E mode would stay the same C is incorrect because the number of particles at E mode and E mean and the value of both would stay the same D is incorrect because E mode and E mean stay the same, but E a will decrease (1) TOTAL FOR SECTION A = 20 MARKS Section B Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • ethanolic (solution) / ethanol as the solvent / ethanolic KOH / ethanolic potassium hydroxide Allow alcohol / alcoholic in place of ethanol / ethanolic Allow in ethanol Allow NaOH / sodium hydroxide in place of KOH / potassium hydroxide Allow aqueous ethanol Ignore concentration of potassium hydroxide Ignore heat / temperatures / pressure Ignore heat under reflux Do not award just ‘ethanol’ or ‘alcohol’ (1) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • elimination (of hydrogen chloride) Do not award nucleophilic elimination (1) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • aqueous (solution) / solution in water / aqueous KOH / aqueous potassium hydroxide Accept aqueous condition / condition is aqueous Allow aqueous ethanol Ignore concentration of potassium hydroxide Ignore temperature Ignore heat under reflux Do not award just ‘water’ (1) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • lone pair on O of OH − • dipole on C‒Cl bond • curly arrow from lone pair on O to C of C‒Cl bond • curly arrow from C‒Cl bond to Cl (or just beyond) All four points scores 2, 2 or 3 points scores 1.
